Removed Code-Review+2 by Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Removed -Code-Review by Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Why TP trivial?  I get this should only show up in interoper but we can test it to be safe - there are other reasons short_io is 0.

Actually, yeah - isn’t there a flag for supporting short io?  0 does not mean not supported by server - it could mean disabled.

At the least the commit message doesn’t match the patch and we should fix that or check for the feature flag if it is indeed *support* rather than “enabled”.

Patch Set 2:

Patrick, if the only objection is to the commit message, then that can be fixed and build quickly killed in Jenkins to avoid retriggering the testing.

The zero-byte-read was only added to refresh the DLM eviction timer on the server so interop testing for this bug would have to be skipped anyway since it was only an issue for clients with 100's of GB RAM caching a single file.  This is expected to be pretty rare with older systems and unlikely to be backported.  If zero-byte reads are disabled for some other reason (e.g. they are causing problems) then still makes sense that the client wouldn't generate any.

Patch Set 2: -Code-Review

Hm.  Well, my objection is that the commit message and the patch don’t match.  When short_io_size is zero, it doesn’t mean it’s unsupported.  It just means it’s off.

And my objection to “trivial” is not about testing for the *bug*, it’s about testing *this patch*.  I understand Maloo isn’t going to hit this bug, but shouldn’t we test the patch fully?  And if there’s no test which sets short io size to zero, we should add one.

These objections certainly aren’t enough to stop landing the patch if it’s urgent - but they are stuff I think we should resolve.

Patch Set 2:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

> (1 comment)

how do you even disable short io? I checked the short io patch and did not see a way to do it?

Patch Set 2:

Set short io size to 0.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Ah, yuck - sorry, my mistake then
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 1a409a3e6a74685970ee779ebe32917bf51eaf3a by Oleg Drokin

commit.subject: LU-14711 osc: Do not attempt sending empty pages
commit.message:
LU-14711 osc: Do not attempt sending empty pages

Do not crash if trying to send a lock-prolonging emtpy read
to an old server, if the server does not support short reads.
Otherwise the client crashes when access the NULL page.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Fixes: 564070343ac4 ("LU-14711 osc: Notify server if cache discard takes a long time")
Change-Id: Icae7bf3ef16c45d33894b3c5fbac15b1a98c39d9
Signed-off-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/44654
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
